verses.text: The LORD speaks to Moses and Aaron in Egypt: this month shall be the beginning of months for you — the first month of the year. Guidance: God resets time itself for His people. The exodus is not just a deliverance but a new calendar. Some encounters with God require us to start counting again.

The LORD speaks to Moses and Aaron in Egypt: this month shall be the beginning of months for you — the first month of the year. Guidance: God resets time itself for His people. The exodus is not just a deliverance but a new calendar. Some encounters with God require us to start counting again.

On the tenth day of this month, every household shall take a lamb — a male, one year old, without blemish. If the household is too small, they shall share one with the nearest neighbor. Guidance: Salvation in Exodus is not solitary. The lamb belongs to a household, and small households share with neighbors. There is no Passover alone.

On the tenth day of this month, every household shall take a lamb — a male, one year old, without blemish. If the household is too small, they shall share one with the nearest neighbor. Guidance: Salvation in Exodus is not solitary. The lamb belongs to a household, and small households share with neighbors. There is no Passover alone.

On the tenth day of this month, every household shall take a lamb — a male, one year old, without blemish. If the household is too small, they shall share one with the nearest neighbor. Guidance: Salvation in Exodus is not solitary. The lamb belongs to a household, and small households share with neighbors. There is no Passover alone.

Keep the lamb until the fourteenth day. Then the whole assembly shall kill it at twilight. Take some of the blood and strike it on the two doorposts and the upper lintel of the houses where you eat it. Guidance: The blood must be applied — not merely shed. The cross of Christ is not retroactive sentiment; it is active trust placed where the angel can see.

Keep the lamb until the fourteenth day. Then the whole assembly shall kill it at twilight. Take some of the blood and strike it on the two doorposts and the upper lintel of the houses where you eat it. Guidance: The blood must be applied — not merely shed. The cross of Christ is not retroactive sentiment; it is active trust placed where the angel can see.

Eat the flesh that night, roasted, with unleavened bread and bitter herbs. Whatever is left until morning, burn it. Eat with your loins girded, shoes on your feet, staff in your hand — in haste. It is the LORD's Passover. Guidance: Salvation is not leisurely. You eat with your shoes on. The first meal of the redeemed is taken standing up, ready to leave.

It is the LORD's Passover. Guidance: Salvation is not leisurely. You eat with your shoes on. The first meal of the redeemed is taken standing up, ready to leave.

I will pass through Egypt this night and strike every firstborn — man and beast — and judge all the gods of Egypt. The blood shall be a sign on the houses where you are; when I see the blood, I will pass over you. Guidance: The Hebrews were not safer in themselves than the Egyptians — they were safer in their door. What protects you is what God is told to look for.

I will pass through Egypt this night and strike every firstborn — man and beast — and judge all the gods of Egypt. The blood shall be a sign on the houses where you are; when I see the blood, I will pass over you. Guidance: The Hebrews were not safer in themselves than the Egyptians — they were safer in their door. What protects you is what God is told to look for.

This day shall be a memorial for you. You shall keep it as a feast to the LORD throughout your generations — an everlasting ordinance. Guidance: God ties an ordinance to a single night so the memory cannot fade. Make rhythms that force you to remember the moment God acted on your behalf.

Seven days you shall eat unleavened bread. Put away all leaven from your houses; whoever eats leaven shall be cut off. The first and seventh days are holy assemblies. Observe this feast — on this day I brought your armies out of Egypt. Guidance: God makes the rescue physical — every meal for a week is a sermon. The yeast of slavery has no place in the new life.

Seven days you shall eat unleavened bread. Put away all leaven from your houses; whoever eats leaven shall be cut off. The first and seventh days are holy assemblies. Observe this feast — on this day I brought your armies out of Egypt. Guidance: God makes the rescue physical — every meal for a week is a sermon. The yeast of slavery has no place in the new life.

Seven days you shall eat unleavened bread. Put away all leaven from your houses; whoever eats leaven shall be cut off. The first and seventh days are holy assemblies. Observe this feast — on this day I brought your armies out of Egypt. Guidance: God makes the rescue physical — every meal for a week is a sermon. The yeast of slavery has no place in the new life.

From the fourteenth day at evening to the twenty-first at evening, you shall eat unleavened bread. For seven days no leaven shall be found in your houses, whether you are a foreigner or native of the land. Eat nothing leavened; in all your dwellings, eat unleavened bread. Guidance: The boundary is firm. There are seasons in the spiritual life where small compromises must be entirely cleared, not negotiated down.

From the fourteenth day at evening to the twenty-first at evening, you shall eat unleavened bread. For seven days no leaven shall be found in your houses, whether you are a foreigner or native of the land. Eat nothing leavened; in all your dwellings, eat unleavened bread. Guidance: The boundary is firm. There are seasons in the spiritual life where small compromises must be entirely cleared, not negotiated down.

Moses calls the elders: take a lamb, kill the passover. Dip a bunch of hyssop in the blood; strike the lintel and two doorposts. None shall go out until morning. The LORD will pass through; when He sees the blood, He will pass over the door, and the destroyer will not enter. Guidance: The blood goes up and around the door — covered above, on both sides. Stay inside the place God has marked.

The LORD will pass through; when He sees the blood, He will pass over the door, and the destroyer will not enter. Guidance: The blood goes up and around the door — covered above, on both sides. Observe this as an ordinance forever. When your children ask, what does this service mean? — say: it is the sacrifice of the LORD's passover, who passed over our houses when He struck the Egyptians. The people bowed and worshipped. Guidance: Faith is meant to be questioned by the next generation. Anticipate the question; have the answer ready. Make sure your worship leaves something for them to ask.

The children of Israel went and did so. As the LORD commanded Moses and Aaron, so they did. Guidance: One short verse, but it carries everything. Salvation in this chapter happens at the speed of obedience. They didn't argue, they didn't delay — they did it.

At midnight the LORD struck every firstborn in Egypt — from Pharaoh on the throne to the captive in the dungeon, all firstborn cattle. Pharaoh rose in the night, his servants, all Egypt. There was a great cry — not a house without one dead. Guidance: Sin's reach is total. Only the blood-marked door stood between any household and the same cry.

At midnight the LORD struck every firstborn in Egypt — from Pharaoh on the throne to the captive in the dungeon, all firstborn cattle. Pharaoh rose in the night, his servants, all Egypt. There was a great cry — not a house without one dead. Guidance: Sin's reach is total. Only the blood-marked door stood between any household and the same cry.

Pharaoh called Moses and Aaron by night: rise up, get you forth from among my people, both you and the children of Israel. Go, serve the LORD as you have said. Take your flocks and herds, as you have said, and be gone — and bless me also. Guidance: The same king who refused for ten plagues now begs to be blessed. Pride breaks in the dark hour. The cost was nine plagues higher than it needed to be.

Pharaoh called Moses and Aaron by night: rise up, get you forth from among my people, both you and the children of Israel. Go, serve the LORD as you have said. Take your flocks and herds, as you have said, and be gone — and bless me also. Guidance: The same king who refused for ten plagues now begs to be blessed. Pride breaks in the dark hour. The cost was nine plagues higher than it needed to be.

The Egyptians urged the people out — we are all dead men. The people took their dough before it leavened, with kneading troughs on their shoulders. They asked the Egyptians for jewels of silver, gold, clothing — the LORD gave them favor. They spoiled the Egyptians. Guidance: God arranges restitution in a single night. Centuries of unpaid labor are settled the moment the people walk out.

The children of Israel journeyed from Rameses to Succoth — about six hundred thousand men on foot, besides children. A mixed multitude went up with them, with flocks and herds. They baked unleavened cakes from the dough they carried, for they were driven out in haste. Guidance: The promise made to one man, Abraham, walks out as a nation — and a mixed crowd of Egyptian believers walks out with them. God's deliverance always sweeps up more than it counted.

The sojourning of the children of Israel in Egypt was four hundred and thirty years. On the very same day at the end of those years, all the hosts of the LORD went out from Egypt. It is a night to be much observed — by all the children of Israel in their generations. Guidance: God keeps appointments four centuries in the making. Watch how He honors His timing, even when the wait was long.

The LORD: this is the ordinance of the passover — no foreigner shall eat of it. But every man's servant bought for money, once circumcised, may eat. A sojourner and a hired servant shall not eat. Guidance: The Passover meal is for those who have been brought under the covenant. Not exclusive by birth — by sign. The door is open, but it is a door, not the absence of a wall.

The LORD: this is the ordinance of the passover — no foreigner shall eat of it. But every man's servant bought for money, once circumcised, may eat. A sojourner and a hired servant shall not eat. Guidance: The Passover meal is for those who have been brought under the covenant. Not exclusive by birth — by sign. The door is open, but it is a door, not the absence of a wall.

The LORD: this is the ordinance of the passover — no foreigner shall eat of it. But every man's servant bought for money, once circumcised, may eat. A sojourner and a hired servant shall not eat. Guidance: The Passover meal is for those who have been brought under the covenant. Not exclusive by birth — by sign. The door is open, but it is a door, not the absence of a wall.

In one house it shall be eaten — not carried outside, not a bone broken. All Israel shall keep it. When a stranger sojourns with you and would keep the passover, let his males be circumcised; then he may come near, as one born in the land. One law for native and stranger. Guidance: The unbroken bones foreshadow the Lamb of God whose bones would not be broken. Foreigners are welcome on the same terms — God's covenant is open to anyone willing to be marked.

All the children of Israel did so — as the LORD commanded Moses and Aaron, so they did. On that very same day, the LORD brought the children of Israel out of the land of Egypt by their armies. Guidance: The chapter that began with one lamb in each home ends with an entire nation marching out. Faithful obedience in the smallest unit produces the largest movement.
